title: Assays of nucleosome assembly and the inhibition of histone acetyltransferase activity. (3) Preparation of nuclei from HeLa cells

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bpglp","text":"・　Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS; pH 7.4) \r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ynwvs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2xxdg","text":"・　Nucleus-isolation buffer: 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.5), 1.5 mM MgCl2, 1.0 mM CaCl2, 0.25 M sucrose (Wako Pure Chemical Industries, Ltd.; cat. no. 196-00018), 0.1 mM phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF; Sigma-Aldrich Japan; cat. no. L3771) \r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":64,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":78,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"89xre","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"irr8u","text":"・　20 % (v/v) Triton X-100 (Sigma-Aldrich Corp.; cat. no. 93426) \r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2rgwj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"babsx","text":"・　Saturated solution of NaCl and urea [5 M NaCl, 8 M urea (Sigma-Aldrich Corp.; cat. no. 02493; stirred overnight and stored at room temperature)] \r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nm7ol","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"yr8tx","text":"・　Dounce homogenizer (B pestle; Wheaton Science Products, Millville, NJ, U.S.A.; cat. no. 357542)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Harvest HeLa S3 cells from a culture of approximately 5 x 10 5 cells/mL in a 2-L spinner flask by centrifugation at 500 x g for 10 min at 4 °C.
Wash the cell pellet by gentle resuspension in ice-cold PBS and repeat the centrifugation as above.

Wash the cell pellet again with ice-cold nucleus-isolation buffer and resuspend the cell pellet by gentle agitation in 50 mL of ice-cold nucleus-isolation buffer supplemented with 0.5% (v/v) Triton X-100. Allow the cells to swell for 10 min on ice.

Homogenize the swollen cells on ice with 15 strokes of a Dounce homogenizer on ice. Pour the suspension of homogenized cells (about 60 mL) into two 50-mL conical tubes and centrifuge them at 500 x g for 5 min at 4 °C.

Pour off cloudy supernatants slowly and then gently resuspend each nuclear pellet in approximately 5 mL of nucleus-isolation buffer plus Triton X-100. Add nucleus-isolation buffer to bring the suspension to the original volume (approximately 30 mL per tube) and pellet the nuclei again by centrifugation at 500 x g for 5 min at 4 °C.
Repeat step 5, at least twice, until the nuclei are pure white and the supernatant is clear.

Resuspend each nuclear pellet gently in approximately 5 mL of nucleus-isolation buffer and combine the suspensions in one conical tube. Adjust the total volume to approximately 20 mL with nucleus-isolation buffer. Remove a 5-µL aliquot and measure the absorbance at 260 nm in 1 mL of a saturated solution of NaCl and urea, using this solution as a blank, to quantify the amount of chromatin DNA (20 OD 260nm = 1 mg/mL DNA). The total yield should be close to 30 mg of chromatin as DNA. Pellet the nuclei by centrifugation at 10,000 x g and carefully remove the supernatant. Freeze the nuclear pellet in liquid nitrogen and store at –80 °C.

Regulation of histone acetylation and nucleosome assembly by transcription factor JDP2 by Chunyuan Jin, Kohsuke Kato, Takahiko Chimura, Takahito Yamasaki, Koji Nakade, Takehide Murata, Hongjie Li, Jianzhi Pan, Mujun Zhao, Kailai Sun, Robert Chiu, Takashi Ito, Kyosuke Nagata, Masami Horikoshi & Kazunari K Yokoyama published in Nature Structural & Molecular Biology on 24 Jul, 2007 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nsmb1063
